untranslatable_reason: Signal requires (1) dynamic normalized deviation calculation (z-score of price relative to moving average), (2) position sizing inversely proportional to deviation magnitude, and (3) mean reversion half-life calculation via regression. These quantitative mechanics cannot be expressed using only binary entry/exit conditions with the allowed indicators and operators. The signal is fundamentally about position scaling and statistical mean reversion detection, not discrete buy/sell triggers.
translation_notes: This signal describes a sophisticated quantitative strategy requiring real-time calculation of standardized deviations, dynamic position sizing based on continuous deviation magnitude, and regression-based half-life estimation. A simplified approximation using sma_20 crossovers captures direction reversal but loses the core mean-reversion position-scaling logic. Author provided minimal parameter guidance (AUTHOR_SILENT on lookback, threshold, sharpe, period). The ~115 day half-life suggests a longer-term mean reversion framework inappropriate for short holding periods.
